ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region the country belongs to */,
  "happiness_rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the country in terms of happiness */,
  "happiness_score" REAL /* Score of the country in terms of happiness */,
  "lower_confidence_interval" REAL /* Lower confidence interval for the happiness score */,
  "upper_confidence_interval" REAL /* Upper confidence interval for the happiness score */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "gdp" REAL /* GDP of the country */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the country data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the country data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_countries_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Western Europe) */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the metric belongs to */,
  "economy_gdp_per_capita" REAL /* Economy (GDP per capita) metric */,
  "family" REAL /* Family metric */,
  "health_life_expectancy" REAL /* Health (Life Expectancy) metric */,
  "freedom" REAL /* Freedom metric */,
  "trust_government_corruption" REAL /* Trust (Government Corruption) metric */,
  "generosity" REAL /* Generosity metric */,
  "dystopia_residual" REAL /* Dystopia residual metric */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the metric was recorded */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., World Bank, Gallup) */,
  "data_source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "data_source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metric_data_sources" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id", "data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)
